name: fastapi
description: FastAPI framework power pack — Pydantic validation, dependency injection, async correctness, OpenAPI hygiene, and security middleware
rules: Always type request/response bodies with Pydantic models
Use Depends() for auth, DB sessions, and settings — never globals
Async endpoints only when actually doing async I/O
Add response_model and status_code to every route for accurate OpenAPI
Pin route order: specific paths before catch-alls
Use APIRouter to organize endpoints by domain, never one giant app.py
Validate path/query params with typed annotations + Field constraints
Use BackgroundTasks or a real queue (Celery/RQ/arq) for fire-and-forget work
Configure CORS explicitly — never allow_origins=['*'] with credentials
Wrap blocking I/O in run_in_threadpool or asyncio.to_thread

checks.description: CORS allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is a security vulnerability
Route declared without response_model — OpenAPI/clients will treat as Any
Synchronous requests/time.sleep/open inside async def blocks the event loop
FastAPI(debug=True) leaks tracebacks — never enable in production
POST/PUT endpoint accepting raw dict/Any instead of Pydantic model
Mutating route (POST/PUT/DELETE) without a Depends() — likely missing auth
Hardcoded SECRET_KEY/JWT signing key — must come from env
TrustedHostMiddleware with allowed_hosts=['*'] disables host header validation
Exception handler catching base Exception without re-raise hides bugs
@app.on_event is deprecated — use lifespan context manager
